instruction.imperative: Do not let frontend fallback logic recompute vowel-family truth when backend resonance data already exists.
forbiddenDrift: Do not patch only the visual color palette
Do not bypass backend resonance gates
Do not trust frontend-only phoneme analysis over backend truth
requiredChecks: Verify backend resonance indices are passed to frontend
Verify frontend renders confirmed indices only
Run rhyme coloring regression tests

english.shortMeaning: Frontend rhyme coloring bug caused by weaker local vowel-family fallback.
english.expandedMeaning: This pattern indicates a desync between backend phoneme authority and frontend rendering logic. The backend should remain the source of truth for vowel-family and resonance decisions. The frontend may render confirmed indices but should not independently recalculate linguistic truth.
english.operatorInstruction: When this gene activates, inspect backend-to-frontend resonance data flow before changing UI color logic.

instruction.imperative: Backend computation and canonical data are the source of truth. Frontend display must reflect backend state, never recompute it.
forbiddenDrift: Do not implement duplicate logic in frontend
Do not treat frontend cache as authoritative
Do not bypass backend validation gates
requiredChecks: Confirm backend endpoint returns computed value
Confirm frontend consumes value without transformation
Verify no shadow computation exists in UI layer

english.shortMeaning: Backend truth authority: the server owns canonical state and computation.
english.expandedMeaning: This architectural rule prevents frontend drift by declaring that authoritative logic lives in backend services. The UI layer renders and reacts; it does not independently calculate combat, scoring, or phoneme truth.
english.operatorInstruction: When this gene activates, route authority questions to backend code and tests, not frontend components.

instruction.imperative: Do not assume memory state. Search the codebase or memory substrate before declaring a fact about implementation.
forbiddenDrift: Do not answer from model memory alone when code truth is available
Do not skip search because the answer feels obvious
Do not cite unverified assumptions as facts
requiredChecks: Run a targeted search for the symbol or pattern
Cross-check with at least one source file or memory record
Record the evidence reference in the context ledger

english.shortMeaning: Search before assume: verify claims against substrate before acting.
english.expandedMeaning: This rule guards against hallucinated implementation details by requiring evidence retrieval before confident statements about code, memory, or system behavior.
english.operatorInstruction: When this gene activates, require a search step and record evidence before accepting the task's premises.

instruction.imperative: When forging or recalling the Holy Fire Claymore, load skeleton holy_fire_claymore.from_image.v1 (PBRAIN-v1, 64x128, 2322 coords, 32 colors) and render the sprite from its coordinates; preserve the vertical symmetry axis at x=31.5.
forbiddenDrift: Do not derive canonical state from the raster preview.
requiredChecks: Verify FNV-1a-32 checksum 6DB23A1A before use.
Preserve vertical symmetry axis x=31.5.

english.shortMeaning: Holy Fire Claymore sprite skeleton: 64x128 traced PixelBrain asset; render from coordinates and keep vertical symmetry.
english.expandedMeaning: Traced from claymore_holy_fire_upscale; 13 semantic parts (blade_core/edge, crossguard, grip_wrap, holy_fire, embers, pommel). Symmetry score 0.84.
english.operatorInstruction: When forging or recalling the Holy Fire Claymore, load skeleton holy_fire_claymore.from_image.v1 (PBRAIN-v1, 64x128, 2322 coords, 32 colors) and render the sprite from its coordinates; preserve the vertical symmetry axis at x=31.5.

instruction.imperative: When forging or recalling the Holy Fire Claymore, load skeleton holy_fire_claymore.from_image.v1 (PBRAIN-v1, 64x128, 2322 coords, 32 colors) and render the sprite from its coordinates; preserve the vertical symmetry axis at x=31.5.
forbiddenDrift: Do not derive canonical state from the raster preview.
requiredChecks: Verify FNV-1a-32 checksum 6DB23A1A with vaelrix_forcefield.pixelbrain.pbrain_checksum (Python json.dumps(packet-minus-checksum, separators=(',',':'))). JS verifiers fail: JSON.parse collapses float 64.0 to 64.
Preserve vertical symmetry axis x=31.5.

english.shortMeaning: Holy Fire Claymore sprite skeleton: 64x128 traced PixelBrain asset; render from coordinates and keep vertical symmetry.
english.expandedMeaning: Traced from claymore_holy_fire_upscale; 13 semantic parts. Checksum is Python-compact FNV-1a-32; JS verifiers fail on float 64.0.
english.operatorInstruction: When forging or recalling the Holy Fire Claymore, load skeleton holy_fire_claymore.from_image.v1 (PBRAIN-v1, 64x128, 2322 coords, 32 colors) and render the sprite from its coordinates; preserve the vertical symmetry axis at x=31.5.

instruction.imperative: When forging or recalling the Void Ice Claymore, load skeleton void_ice_claymore.morph.v1 (PBRAIN-v1, 64x128, 2322 coords, 24 colors) and render from its coordinates; it is a luminance-ramp morph of holy_fire_claymore (icy_fire flames, sapphire blade, voidsteel hilt, amethyst pommel). Preserve vertical symmetry axis x=31.5.
forbiddenDrift: Do not derive canonical state from the raster preview.
requiredChecks: Verify FNV-1a-32 checksum 1A72809C with vaelrix_forcefield.pixelbrain.pbrain_checksum (Python json.dumps(packet-minus-checksum, separators=(',',':'))).
Preserve vertical symmetry axis x=31.5.

english.shortMeaning: Void Ice Claymore: ice-flame + void recolor morph of the Holy Fire Claymore; render from coordinates, keep vertical symmetry.
english.expandedMeaning: Per-part luminance-preserving ramp remap onto material-registry void/ice materials. Geometry identical to holy_fire_claymore.from_image.v1.
english.operatorInstruction: When forging or recalling the Void Ice Claymore, load skeleton void_ice_claymore.morph.v1 (PBRAIN-v1, 64x128, 2322 coords, 24 colors) and render from its coordinates; it is a luminance-ramp morph of holy_fire_claymore (icy_fire flames, sapphire blade, voidsteel hilt, amethyst pommel). Preserve vertical symmetry axis x=31.5.

instruction.imperative: When handling mathematical stroke formulas or Wand propagation tasks, treat the formula as a chemical reaction law. Do not perform culinary batch sampling or parameter jitter. Instead: 1) Parse the stroke formula's parametric kinetics (path, width(t), pressure, bleed). 2) Propagate by recursive generative unfolding using PixelBrain/SCDL laws as reaction mechanisms (symmetry as replication catalyst, material resonance as reagent selection, vector/ring/path ops as bonding, TurboQuant as crystallization/quantization step, photonic scoring as selection pressure). 3) Output expanded geometry or sub-formulas that self-organize according to the original law. Prioritize deterministic rule application over trial-and-error variations.
forbiddenDrift: Do not implement propagation as repeated calls with small random param tweaks
Do not rely on score-and-pick from multiple independent evaluations
Do not treat the stroke as a static recipe to be varied and tasted
Do not bypass the formula's own math in favor of external heuristics
requiredChecks: Verify that propagation applies the original formula's functions recursively or via field rules
Confirm use of symmetry, material, and SCDL-compatible ops as the propagation operators
Ensure TurboQuant and photonic mechanisms are used for state selection, not just post-filtering
Validate output remains expressible as efficient SCDL or PixelBrain packet

english.shortMeaning: Wand stroke formulas must propagate chemically via generative rules, not culinarily via batch sampling.
english.expandedMeaning: The Wand's core power is authoring pure mathematical models of strokes. Propagation must honor that by letting the model act as a reaction law that unfolds deterministically using the engine's existing symmetries, quantizers, and perception systems. This produces self-consistent, evolvable art instead of ad-hoc variations.
english.operatorInstruction: When this gene is active for a prompt involving Wand, stroke formulas, propagation, tactical art assets, or procedural pixel generation: redirect any 'try variations' thinking toward recursive application of the formula's math under PixelBrain laws. Use the formula parameters as kinetics, not as knobs for manual experimentation.

instruction.imperative: A SemanticAct's kind is its illocutionary type. It is NOT its permission. Permission lives in law.decision and nowhere else.
forbiddenDrift: Do not add Forbidden or Escalate to CalculusKind - they are law.decision values, not act types
Do not read kind to decide whether something may execute; read kind AND law.decision AND capability
Do not let a policy verdict change the kind - a blocked delete is still a Do that LAW refused
requiredChecks: Assert CalculusKind contains no policy verdict
Assert the executor checks kind === 'Do' && law.decision === 'allow' && capability
Assert no act can be constructed with kind='Do' and law.decision='block' (illegal state must be unrepresentable)

english.shortMeaning: Kind answers 'what kind of speech act is this'. law.decision answers 'may it happen'. Two axes, never one enum.
english.expandedMeaning: Measured 2026-07-16: two annotators labelling 200 UI intents scored Cohen's kappa 0.271. The dominant split was 'delete the album' -> Do (act type) vs Escalate (policy). Both readings were correct on different axes, because CalculusKind conflated them: Forbidden and Escalate duplicated law.decision's 'block' and 'escalate' inside the kind enum. That made illegal states representable (kind='Do' with law='block') and made the taxonomy unannotatable, because an annotator must silently pick which axis to project onto.
english.operatorInstruction: If you are about to add a kind, ask: is this a TYPE of utterance, or a VERDICT about one? Verdicts go in law.

instruction.imperative: Theory means exactly one thing: the utterance names a concept with no binding in the executable lexicon. It is a lookup, not a judgement.
forbiddenDrift: Do not use Theory as a catch-all for 'the annotator felt unsure'
Do not emit Theory for a bound concept whose slots are merely missing - that is Clarify
Do not treat Theory as fact, and never let an unpromoted Theory reach an executor
Do not resolve an unbound concept to a plausible default - `|| 'stone'` is the null this gene forbids
requiredChecks: Assert Theory is emitted iff bindExact() returns undefined
Assert Theory is never executable (assertExecutable throws)
Assert an unbound utterance deposits to the theory bank before the final kind is sealed

english.expandedMeaning: Theory's frequency is a function of lexicon coverage, not of utterance difficulty. Measured 2026-07-16: the codebase author used Theory on 1 of 200 UI intents because he carries the Scholomance lexicon in his head, so nothing misses; the compiler binds 11 phrases, so nearly everything misses. The same utterance is Theory to the machine and Do to the author. This is why Theory cannot be established by human annotation (kappa -0.013): it is not a property of the utterance, it is a property of the utterance RELATIVE TO A LEXICON VERSION. Cite this gene and the lexicon version together, or the deposit is unreproducible.
english.operatorInstruction: When a Theory deposit looks wrong, check the lexicon version before questioning the concept.

instruction.imperative: Probe means the bound operation is read-only: it may gather evidence and must commit nothing.
forbiddenDrift: Do not emit Probe for anything whose formula declares a mutating effect
Do not let a Probe carry a capability that permits writes
Do not treat 'answering a question' as automatically read-only if answering requires a fetch that mutates state
requiredChecks: Assert Probe is emitted iff the bound formation formula declares effect: 'read'
Assert a Probe act's capability scope contains no write verbs

english.shortMeaning: Probe = bound + read-only effect. Derived from the formula's declared effect, not from the utterance's grammar.
english.expandedMeaning: Probe scored kappa 0.170 between two annotators, with 14 Probe->Do swaps, because 'is answering a question itself an action?' is not decidable from the utterance. It IS decidable from the formula: the formation formula declares whether it mutates. Read the formula, not the sentence.
english.operatorInstruction: If you cannot tell whether something is a Probe, you are looking at the utterance. Look at the formula.

instruction.imperative: Clarify means the formula bound but a required slot is unresolved, so exactly one bounded question would ground it.
forbiddenDrift: Do not emit Clarify when nothing bound - that is Theory
Do not emit Clarify when the question would be unbounded ('what do you mean?') - that is Escalate via law
Do not resolve a missing slot to a default and emit Do - the soft Do is the failure this whole architecture exists to prevent
requiredChecks: Assert Clarify is emitted iff a bound formula has >=1 unresolved required slot
Assert the emitted question names the missing slot
Assert formation formulas declare their required slots, or Clarify is not computable

english.shortMeaning: Clarify = bound formula + missing required slot. Computable ONLY if formulas declare required slots.
english.expandedMeaning: Clarify scored kappa -0.087 - worse than chance - the worst kind in the taxonomy. Cause: 'narrowed enough to ask a bounded question' is prose, and deixis ('open it', 'close that') resolves instantly for anyone holding the app's state and not at all for anyone who is not. This gene makes Clarify computable by moving the test from the annotator's intuition to the formula's declared slot schema. UNTIL every formation formula declares requiredSlots, Clarify remains a judgement call and this gene is aspirational.
english.operatorInstruction: If Clarify is firing unpredictably, the formula is missing a requiredSlots declaration.

instruction.imperative: Do means bound + every required slot resolved + a mutating effect. Do is an act TYPE and confers no authority by itself.
forbiddenDrift: Do not treat kind='Do' as permission to execute - check law.decision and capability
Do not emit Do when a required slot is missing - that is Clarify
Do not widen a Do's capability scope from untrusted context
requiredChecks: Assert Do is emitted iff bound && all required slots resolved && effect is mutating
Assert every Do carries a capability with a narrow scope and logical expiry
Assert assertExecutable rejects Do when law.decision !== 'allow'

english.shortMeaning: Do = bound + grounded + mutating. Permission is a separate check.
english.expandedMeaning: A valid act is not a permitted act. This is the F14 capability rule restated as a gene, and it is the direct consequence of SEMANTIC_ACT_KIND_IS_NOT_PERMISSION: once kind stops encoding permission, kind='Do' becomes a claim about grammar, and the executor must consult law.decision and capability before doing anything at all.
english.operatorInstruction: A Do that executes without a capability check is a bug, not a shortcut.

instruction.imperative: Hypothesis means the utterance supplies a CANDIDATE BINDING for a term that does not bind. Theory is an unbound term with no candidate; Hypothesis is an unbound term whose candidate the speaker provided.
forbiddenDrift: Do not emit Hypothesis from a hedge alone - 'not sure what a session word is' is uncertainty with no candidate, which is Theory
Do not emit Hypothesis for a bound term - if it binds, the speaker's proposal is redundant and the act is Do or Clarify
Do not treat a Hypothesis as fact, and never promote one to lexicon without human authority (F10)
Do not let the compiler invent the candidate - the candidate must come from the utterance, or it is the machine guessing and calling it the user's idea
requiredChecks: Assert Hypothesis is emitted iff the term is unbound AND the utterance carries an explicit candidate binding
Assert an unbound utterance with no candidate falls back to Theory (fail-closed)
Assert the deposited Hypothesis records the candidate binding verbatim, so a reviewer can test it
Assert Hypothesis is never executable (assertExecutable throws)

english.shortMeaning: Hypothesis = unbound term + a candidate binding the speaker supplied. It is a Theory that arrives with its own testable answer.
english.expandedMeaning: Ruled an ACT by the repo owner, 2026-07-16. The trigger is a candidate binding: 'X means Y', 'treat X as Y', 'call X a Y', 'X might be Y'. Measured on the 200-item Phase 0.5 corpus, that trigger scored 100% precision and 65% recall against one annotator's Hypothesis labels - it never fires falsely, and the misses (hedged proposals without an explicit X-is-Y structure) fall back to Theory, which is the safe direction. RECORDED DISAGREEMENT: the repo owner labelled 8 of the 11 trigger hits as Do, reading 'treat the fingerprint as an id' as an instruction to comply with rather than a proposal to evaluate. He nonetheless ruled Hypothesis an act. Both readings are defensible and the tension is unresolved: if a user proposing a binding expects the system to ADOPT it, Hypothesis is closer to a Do against the lexicon than to a Theory deposit. Revisit when a second human annotator exists. Hypothesis scored the highest inter-annotator agreement of all seven kinds (kappa 0.505), so the distinction is real even where its consequence is not settled.
english.operatorInstruction: When reviewing a Hypothesis deposit, the candidate binding is already in the record - test it, do not re-derive it. If there is no candidate, the deposit was misfiled and belongs in Theory.
